title_en: 5. Would you like to meet him outside the institution? (cont.)
title_id: 5. Maukah Kamu Bertemu Dengannya di Luar Lembaga? (lanjutan)
content_en: There came to him those who had lagged behind, making excuses for their actions and swearing oaths. They were about eighty-odd people. The Messenger of Allah accepted their excuses at face value and sought forgiveness for them, leaving their innermost secrets to Allah. Be concerned about others Ka'b bin Malik also came. When he greeted him, the Prophet looked at him. He smiled at him, the smile of one who is angry. Ka'b walked towards him and sat in front of him. The Prophet said to him: "What made you lag behind? Did you not buy a ride for yourself?" Meaning: your riding beast. He replied: 'Indeed, I did!' The Prophet asked: "Then, what kept you behind?" Ka'b said: "O Messenger of Allah, if I were to have sat in front of anyone in the world other than you, I would have saved myself from his anger by making an excuse. I have been given the power of persuasion. But by Allah, I know that if I were to tell you a lie today in order to attain your pleasure, then Allah would soon make you angry with me anyway, and if I were to speak the truth, then surely you would be upset with me, but I would hope that because I spoke the truth, Allah would forgive me. O Messenger of Allah, by Allah, I do not have any excuse. By Allah, I had never been stronger nor wealthier than I was when I remained behind!" Ka'b remained silent. Thereupon, the Prophet turned to his Companions and said: "He has indeed spoken the truth. Get up (O Ka'b) and wait until Allah decides your case." Ka'b got up and left the mosque, dragging his feet, feeling remorse and being grief stricken, not knowing what Allah would decide for him. When the people saw this, a group from amongst them followed him and began to censure him. They said: "By Allah! We have not known you to have committed a sin before this. Though, you are a poet, you failed to make excuses to the Messenger of Allah like the rest of those who lagged behind! You could have made an excuse by which he would have been pleased with you and sought forgiveness for you, so that Allah may have forgiven Be concerned about others you.' Ka'b said: "They continued to censure me so much that I considered returning to the Prophet to inform him that I had lied. But I then asked: 'Is there anyone else who has had the same experience?' Someone replied: 'Yes. Two men also said what you said and were told what you were told.' I asked: 'Who are they?' They said: 'Murarah bin al-Rabi' and Hilal bin Umayyah.' These were two righteous men who had witnessed the battle of Badr − men whom I thought could be examples for me. I therefore said: 'By Allah, I will never return to the Prophet to retract what I have said and belie myself!" Thus Ka'b walked along, broken inside and full of sadness, until he reached home, where he remained. Not much time had passed after this when the Prophet forbade the people from speaking to Ka'b and his two Companions. Ka'b said: "After this, people completely changed their attitude towards us and would shun us. If I were to go out to the marketplace, no one would speak to me. The people became estranged from me as if I never knew them. It was as though even the walls had become estranged from us, as if these weren't the walls we were familiar with and the earth that we knew had also become a stranger. The two companions of mine would sit in their homes and weep night and day. They would never show their faces. They would busy themselves with worship as if they were monks. As for me, then I was the youngest and the ﬁrmest of the three. I would go out and attend the prayers with the Muslims and roam around the marketplaces, yet, nobody would speak to me. Be concerned about others "I would enter the mosque and approach the Messenger of Allah and greet him, then be left wondering to myself if he even moved his lips to return my greeting or not. I would pray close to him, looking at him stealthily. When I became busy with prayer, he would turn to me. When I turned to him, he would turn away from me." Days went by and pain led to more pain. Ka'b was once considered a noble man amongst his people and a leading poet. He was known amongst kings and princes. His poetry circulated amongst the great so much that they would long to meet him. Yet here he was in Madinah amongst his own people, and no one would speak to or even look at him, so much so that the tragedy was gruelling and the estrangement became almost impossible to bear.
content_id: Datanglah kepada beliau orang-orang yang tertinggal, membuat alasan atas tindakan mereka dan bersumpah. Mereka berjumlah sekitar delapan puluh orang lebih. Rasulullah menerima alasan-alasan mereka sebagaimana adanya dan memohonkan ampunan untuk mereka, menyerahkan rahasia terdalam mereka kepada Allah. Ka'b bin Malik juga datang. Ketika ia memberi salam, Nabi memandangnya. Beliau tersenyum kepadanya, senyum seseorang yang sedang marah. Ka'b berjalan ke arahnya dan duduk di hadapannya. Nabi berkata kepadanya: "Apa yang membuatmu tertinggal? Bukankah kamu telah membeli kendaraan untuk dirimu?" Maksudnya: hewan tungganganmu. Ia menjawab: 'Memang, aku membelinya!' Nabi bertanya: "Lalu, apa yang membuatmu tertinggal?" Ka'b berkata: "Wahai Rasulullah, kalaulah aku duduk di hadapan siapa pun di dunia ini selain engkau, tentu aku akan menyelamatkan diriku dari amarahnya dengan membuat alasan. Aku telah dianugerahi kemampuan persuasi. Tetapi demi Allah, aku tahu bahwa jika aku berbohong kepadamu hari ini demi meraih keridhoanmu, maka Allah akan segera membuat engkau marah kepadaku juga, dan jika aku berbicara jujur, maka pasti engkau akan kecewa kepadaku, tetapi aku berharap bahwa karena aku berbicara jujur, Allah akan mengampuniku. Wahai Rasulullah, demi Allah, aku tidak punya alasan apa pun. Demi Allah, aku tidak pernah lebih kuat maupun lebih kaya daripada saat aku tertinggal!" Ka'b pun diam. Lalu Nabi berpaling kepada para sahabatnya dan berkata: "Ia memang telah berkata jujur. Berdirilah (wahai Ka'b) dan tunggulah hingga Allah memutuskan perkaramu." Ka'b pun berdiri dan meninggalkan masjid, menyeret langkahnya, merasakan penyesalan dan kesedihan mendalam, tidak tahu apa yang akan Allah putuskan untuknya. Ketika orang-orang melihat hal ini, sekelompok dari mereka mengikutinya dan mulai mencercanya. Mereka berkata: "Demi Allah! Kami tidak pernah mengetahui kamu melakukan dosa sebelum ini. Padahal kamu adalah seorang penyair, kamu gagal membuat alasan kepada Rasulullah seperti yang dilakukan orang-orang yang tertinggal lainnya! Kamu bisa saja membuat alasan yang membuatnya ridha kepadamu dan memohonkan ampunan untukmu, sehingga Allah mungkin mengampunimu." Ka'b berkata: "Mereka terus-menerus mencercaku hingga aku mempertimbangkan untuk kembali kepada Nabi untuk memberitahunya bahwa aku telah berbohong. Tetapi kemudian aku bertanya: 'Apakah ada orang lain yang mengalami hal yang sama?' Seseorang menjawab: 'Ya. Dua orang juga mengatakan apa yang kamu katakan dan diberitahu apa yang kamu diberitahu.' Aku bertanya: 'Siapa mereka?' Mereka berkata: 'Murarah bin ar-Rabi dan Hilal bin Umayyah.' Mereka adalah dua orang saleh yang telah menyaksikan Perang Badar—orang-orang yang menurutku bisa menjadi teladan bagiku. Oleh karena itu aku berkata: 'Demi Allah, aku tidak akan pernah kembali kepada Nabi untuk mencabut apa yang telah aku katakan dan mendustai diriku sendiri!'" Maka Ka'b berjalan, hancur di dalam dan penuh kesedihan, hingga ia sampai ke rumah, di mana ia tinggal. Tidak lama setelah ini Nabi melarang orang-orang untuk berbicara kepada Ka'b dan dua orang temannya. Ka'b berkata: "Setelah ini, orang-orang sepenuhnya mengubah sikap mereka terhadap kami dan menghindari kami. Jika aku pergi ke pasar, tidak ada yang mau berbicara kepadaku. Orang-orang menjadi asing bagiku seolah-olah aku tidak pernah mengenal mereka. Seolah-olah bahkan dinding-dinding pun menjadi asing bagi kami, seolah-olah ini bukan dinding-dinding yang kami kenal, dan bumi yang kami kenal juga menjadi asing. Kedua temanku akan duduk di rumah mereka dan menangis siang dan malam. Mereka tidak pernah menampakkan wajah mereka. Mereka menyibukkan diri dengan ibadah seolah-olah mereka adalah para rahib. Adapun aku, aku adalah yang termuda dan paling tegar di antara ketiganya. Aku akan keluar dan menghadiri shalat bersama kaum Muslimin serta berkeliling di pasar-pasar, namun tidak ada yang berbicara kepadaku. 'Aku akan masuk ke masjid dan mendekati Rasulullah dan memberi salam kepadanya, lalu bertanya-tanya dalam hati apakah beliau bahkan menggerakkan bibirnya untuk membalas salamku atau tidak. Aku akan shalat dekat dengannya, memandangnya secara diam-diam. Ketika aku sibuk dengan shalat, beliau akan berpaling ke arahku. Ketika aku berpaling ke arahnya, beliau akan berpaling dariku.' Hari demi hari berlalu dan kepedihan demi kepedihan semakin bertambah. Ka'b dulu dianggap sebagai orang mulia di antara kaumnya dan penyair terkemuka. Ia dikenal di kalangan raja-raja dan pangeran-pangeran. Puisi-puisinya beredar di kalangan para pembesar sehingga mereka sangat ingin menemuinya. Namun kini ia berada di Madinah di antara kaumnya sendiri, dan tidak ada seorang pun yang mau berbicara kepadanya atau bahkan memandangnya, sehingga tragedi itu sangat menyakitkan dan keterasingan itu hampir tidak tertahankan.
